raw_consumer_statement:
Barclays failed to conduct a reasonable investigation into my billing dispute involving a {$360.00} hotel charge for services not rendered. 

During a prior stay at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX in XX/XX/XXXX, I made a future reservation for a later trip related to my son XXXX XXXX XXXX games in the XXXX XXXX area. Immediately after checkout on XX/XX/XXXX2025 at XXXX PM, I canceled that future reservation by phone. My phone records confirm that call. 

I canceled because I learned that XXXX team would be staying at XXXX XXXX La XXXX, which was logistically better for team coordination and transportation to the baseball field. I then stayed at XXXX XXXX La XXXX from XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX, and that XXXX confirms my physical presence there during the same period the merchant later claimed I stayed at XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, while I was already staying at XXXX XXXX, I received notice of a no-show charge and immediately contacted XXXX. XXXX issued case number XXXX that night, confirming that I raised the dispute before the hotel later processed the reservation as a completed stay. 

Despite my prior cancellation and that pre-stay notice, XXXX XXXX XXXX later processed the reservation as though I had actually stayed there. The hotel sent a Thank you for staying email reflecting confirmation # XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX check-in, XX/XX/XXXX check-out, and an award of XXXX loyalty points. 

Barclays nevertheless denied my billing dispute after accepting the merchants conclusory statement and internally generated folio. Barclays did not require objective proof of actual service delivery, such as a signed registration card, ID verification, key issuance records, or room access logs. 

This was not a reasonable investigation. Barclays accepted contradictory merchant records at face value even though the documentary record showed : 1. a contemporaneous cancellation call, 2. a same-night XXXX case opened before the hotel finalized the supposed stay, and 3. independent third-party evidence that I was physically staying at another hotel during the same dates.
